{"step_by_step_thinking": "The number needed to treat (NNT) is calculated as the reciprocal of the absolute risk reduction (ARR). The ARR is the difference in event rates between the two treatment groups.

In this study:
- Medication 1 (investigational drug) had 134 deaths from cardiac causes out of 1,500 patients.
- Medication 2 (standard therapy) had 210 deaths from cardiac causes out of 1,500 patients.

Event rate in medication 1 = 134 / 1500 = 0.08933
Event rate in medication 2 = 210 / 1500 = 0.14

Absolute risk reduction (ARR) = 0.14 - 0.08933 = 0.05067

NNT = 1 / ARR = 1 / 0.05067 ≈ 19.74

Rounded to the nearest whole number, this is 20.

Therefore, the correct answer is 20.", "answer_choice": "C"}
